Name: CMMC
Subtitle: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification
Definition:
This framework provides a tiered approach that must be monitored according to NIST SP 800-171,
based on five different levels of maturity expectations.
The Control Families in this framework are intentionally focused on the technical controls specified in NIST 800-171
in support of the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification.

Name: RMF
Subtitle: Risk Management Framework
Definition:
This framework provides an overview of NIST SP 800-53 rev5 Control Families that must be monitored according to DoD Instruction 8510.1,
which establishes the Risk Management Framework (RMF) for DoD IT.
The Control Families in this framework are intentionally focused on the technical controls specified in SP 800-53 rev5
in support of NIST Risk Management Framework (SP 800-37), both referenced in DoD 8510.1.

Name: FISMA
Subtitle: Federal Information Security Modernization Act
Definition:
This framework an overview of NIST SP 800-53 rev5 Control Families that must be monitored according to FISMA.
The Control Families in this framework intentionally focused on the technical controls specified in SP 800-53 rev5.

Name: DFARS
Subtitle: Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement
Definition:
This framework provides an overview of NIST SP 800-171 rev1 Control Families that must be monitored.
The Control Families in this framework are intentionally focused on the technical controls specified in SP 800-171 rev1.

Name: OMB M-21-31
Subtitle: Office of Management and Budget 21-31 Memorandum
Definition:
This framework provides an overview of M-21-31 which addresses the requirements and guides the implementation process, enabling organizations to identify and respond to cyber threats effectively.
The Control Families in this framework are intentionally focused on the technical controls specified in OMB M-21-31.

Name: ISM (March 2023)
Subtitle: Information Security Manual (Version March 2023)
Definition: This framework provides an overview of the cyber security framework that an organisation can apply, using their risk management framework, to help protect their systems and data from cyber threats.

Name: ACSC Essential Eight
Subtitle: Australian Cyber Security Centre Essential Eight
Definition: "This framework provides an overview of the Essential Eight framework from the ACSC's Strategies to Mitigate Cyber Security Incidents. The baseline makes it much harder for adversaries to compromise systems."

Name: CAF
Subtitle: Cyber Assessment Framework
Definition: "This framework provides an overview of the Cyber Assessment Framework which provides a systematic and comprehensive approach to assessing the extent to which cyber risks to essential functions are being managed by the organisation responsible."

Name: AESCSF
Subtitle: Australian Energy Sector Cyber Security Framework
Definition: This framework provides an overview of the Australian Energy Sector Cyber Security Framework. The Australian Energy Sector Cyber Security Framework (AESCSF) has been developed through collaboration with industry and government stakeholders, including the Australian Energy Market Operator (AEMO), Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC), Cyber and Infrastructure Security Centre (CISC), and representatives from Australian energy organisations.
